# Browser Use

> Easiest way to automate the web. Tell an agent in natural language what to do, and it interacts with the web like a human.

Browser Use has two products: **Browser Use Cloud** (managed SDK and API) and **Browser Use Open Source** (self-hosted Python library).

## Browser Use Cloud

SDK and API for browser automation with stealth browsers, CAPTCHA solving, residential proxies, and managed infrastructure. Available in Python and TypeScript.

- [Full SDK Reference](https://docs.browser-use.com/cloud/vibecoding): Every method, parameter, and response type in one page.
- [Cloud Documentation](https://docs.browser-use.com): Agent, Browser, Authentication, and more.
- [API v3 Reference](https://docs.browser-use.com/cloud/api-reference): REST API endpoints.

## Browser Use Open Source

Self-hosted Python library. Bring your own LLM and run on your own infrastructure.

- [Open Source Documentation Index](/open-source/llms.txt): Concise page index.
- [Open Source Full Reference](/open-source/llms-full.txt): Complete documentation.

## Quick Start (Cloud)

Install:
- Python: `pip install browser-use-sdk`
- TypeScript: `npm install browser-use-sdk`

Set your API key:
```
export BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=your_key
```
Get a key at: https://cloud.browser-use.com/settings?tab=api-keys&new=1

### Python
```python
import asyncio
from browser_use_sdk import AsyncBrowserUse

async def main():
    client = AsyncBrowserUse()
    result = await client.run("Find the top 3 Hacker News posts")
    print(result.output)

asyncio.run(main())
```

### TypeScript
```typescript
import { BrowserUse } from "browser-use-sdk";

const client = new BrowserUse();
const result = await client.run("Find the top 3 Hacker News posts");
console.log(result.output);
```

### Structured output (Python)
```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from browser_use_sdk import AsyncBrowserUse

class Product(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float

client = AsyncBrowserUse()
result = await client.run("Get product info from amazon.com/dp/...", output_schema=Product)
print(result.output)  # Product(name=..., price=...)
```

### Key run() parameters
- `model` — `"bu-mini"` (default) or `"bu-max"` (more capable)
- `output_schema` / `schema` — Pydantic model or Zod schema for structured output
- `session_id` / `sessionId` — reuse an existing session for follow-up tasks
- `keep_alive` / `keepAlive` — keep session alive after task (default: false)
- `profile_id` / `profileId` — persistent browser profile
- `proxy_country_code` / `proxyCountryCode` — residential proxy country (default: us)
- `workspace_id` / `workspaceId` — workspace for file persistence
- `enable_recording` / `enableRecording` — record session as MP4
- `max_cost_usd` / `maxCostUsd` — cost cap in USD

### Workspaces (file handling)
```python
workspace = await client.workspaces.create(name="my-workspace")
result = await client.run("Save top 3 HN posts as posts.json", workspace_id=str(workspace.id))
files = await client.workspaces.files(str(workspace.id), include_urls=True)
for f in files.files:
    print(f.path, f.url)
```

### Profiles (persistent login)
```python
profile = await client.profiles.create(name="user-id-1")
session = await client.sessions.create(profile_id=profile.id)
result = await client.run("Check notifications", session_id=session.id)
```

### Browsers (direct CDP access)
```python
browser = await client.browsers.create(proxy_country_code="us")
# Connect with Playwright/Puppeteer/Selenium via browser.cdp_url
# Or: wss://connect.browser-use.com?apiKey=KEY&proxyCountryCode=us
```

For v2 legacy SDK, see: https://docs.browser-use.com/cloud/legacy/agent

## Quick Start (Open Source)

Install: `pip install browser-use`

```python
from browser_use import Agent, ChatBrowserUse

agent = Agent(
    task="Find the top 3 trending repos on GitHub",
    llm=ChatBrowserUse(),
)
result = await agent.run()
print(result.final_result())
```

## Full SDK Reference

For the complete SDK reference, see:
https://docs.browser-use.com/cloud/vibecoding
